steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that Knight imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which "accounts in part for the impressionistic qualities synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic." The question asks what logically follows from this.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Because of the manner in which they were created, they likely have visual qualities that are regarded as more typical of Florida Highwaymen paintings than the qualities in works by Reagan are.": follows directly — adopting Hair's technique means Knight's works share the group's defining impressionistic traits. ✓
  • "Although it is evident that Knight adopted some of Hair's preferred techniques, Knight's works are less derivative of works by Hair than is typically acknowledged.": introduces an unsupported claim about how derivative Knight's works are; the passage says Knight imitated Hair's technique but does not compare that dependence with what is typically acknowledged. ✗
  • "The lack of precision with which they were executed suggests that they are inferior to works by either Hair or Reagan.": another unsupported quality comparison; the passage describes Reagan's style without ranking it. ✗
  • "Knight's reliance on the technique of fast painting likely accounts for his works being more aesthetically interesting than works by Reagan are.": introduces an unsupported aesthetic ranking; the passage does not say Knight's works are more aesthetically interesting than Reagan's. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
